summary: Tested the new Permissions admin module (backend + frontend). Backend: created /app/backend/tests/test_permissions.py — 13 passed, 1 skipped (in-use guard couldn't seed a user via /api/users in this env, but the code path is trivial and verified by code review). Frontend: confirmed Settings Hub shows the new Permissions card; /admin/permissions renders with 4 system roles (Admin, Manager, Staff, Super Admin), pages grid, group bulk-toggle (Deselect all), search, Save changes, and New Role dialog. Create-role dialog validates required fields with toast 'Both Role ID and Display Name are required'. /admin/supplier-products still works for super_admin with Cost/Live/Status columns visible and all action buttons (50 preview/eye buttons present) — super_admin bypass works as designed.
action_items: Optional: pre-existing React warning 'Each child in a list should have a unique key prop' in AdminDashboard render — unrelated to this task, but worth fixing in a future iteration.

Optional: backend test for delete-role-in-use guard (HTTP 400 when users still hold the role) is currently skipped because the test couldn't create a user via /api/users in this env. Code review shows the guard at routes/permissions.py:381-386 is correct (count_documents on db.users by role). Main agent could expose a small admin user creation helper or a /api/permissions/roles/{role_id}/_test_assign endpoint to make this fully exercisable, but it's not required for shipping.
critical_code_review_comments: permissions.py is well structured: idempotent system-role seed, validates registry keys on POST/PUT, prevents super_admin edit, blocks system-role delete and in-use delete. No ObjectId leakage (uses projection {_id: 0} on reads).
Default OFF behaviour for new custom roles is correctly enforced by the registry-key intersection in create_role (pages and actions filtered against PAGE_REGISTRY/ACTION_REGISTRY).

Minor: SYSTEM_ROLE_DEFAULTS is only seeded once. If new pages or actions are added to PAGE_REGISTRY/ACTION_REGISTRY later, existing seeded admin/manager/staff rows will not pick them up automatically. This is by design (default OFF for new permissions) and matches the user's stated requirement, but worth a one-line comment in the file.
Minor: in PUT /roles/{role_id}, role_name on system roles is silently ignored (good), but no 200/error signal back to the UI. Acceptable.
PermissionsContext / PermissionsAdmin frontend integration looks solid — observed roles list loaded immediately after navigation.

success_rate.backend: 100% (13/13 passing, 1 skipped due to env limitation)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (all UI elements render and behave correctly for the super_admin happy-path)

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ prefixed roles during testing and cleaned them up via TestCleanup. System now has the original 4 system roles only.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend permissions API is solid. If a future change touches role assignment, re-enable test_delete_role_in_use_returns_400 by providing a way to create a user with a custom role. Frontend regression coverage for non-super-admin (lock screen) was not tested because admin/manager/staff seed users were not provisioned in this env — main agent should manually verify the lock screen for at least the 'admin' role if they have or create such a user.
